heading: Suspension of sanctions and other measures
label: 9251 Suspension of sanctions and other measures
notes: References in TextSubchapter I, II, or III (or any amendment made by such subchapters), referred to in subsec. (a), was in the original “title I, II, or III (or any amendment made by such titles)”, meaning titles I, II, and III of , , , 101, 112. Titles I and III are classified principally to subchapters I and III, respectively, of this chapter. Title II is classified generally to subchapter II of this chapter. children.text: verifiably ceasing its counterfeiting of United States currency, including the surrender or destruction of specialized materials and equipment used or particularly suitable for counterfeiting;
taking steps toward financial transparency to comply with generally accepted protocols to cease and prevent the laundering of monetary instruments;
taking steps toward verification of its compliance with applicable United Nations Security Council resolutions;

children.label: A
children.text: abducted or unlawfully held captive by the Government of North Korea; or

children.label: B
children.text: detained in violation of the Agreement Concerning a Military Armistice in Korea, signed at Panmunjom (commonly referred to as the “Korean War Armistice Agreement”);July 27, 1953
accepting and beginning to abide by internationally recognized standards for the distribution and monitoring of humanitarian aid; and
taking verified steps to improve living conditions in its political prison camps.

children.heading: Renewal of suspension
children.label: b Renewal of suspension
children.text: The suspension described in subsection (a) may be renewed for additional, consecutive 180-day periods after the President certifies to the appropriate congressional committees that the Government of North Korea has continued to comply with the conditions described in subsection (a) during the previous year.
